[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: cascade tracing; directed dependency graph; critical path quantification; counterfactual node test
Suppress: isolated failure assumption; cascade blindness; sequential assumption without verifying parallel branches
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

---

## Solution

**Step 1 — Initial state at t=0 (Cable 1 snaps)**

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Remaining cables | 5 |
| Load per cable | 47 ÷ 5 = **9.4 tons** |
| Capacity per cable | **10 tons** |
| Load > 9 tons? | Yes → fatigue active |
| Fatigue rate | −0.5 tons/hour capacity loss |

---

**Step 2 — Model capacity degradation**

All 5 cables are in identical states: same load (9.4 t), same starting capacity (10 t), same degradation rate.

```
Capacity(t) = 10 − 0.5t     [t in hours]
```

A cable fails when its capacity equals its load:

```
10 − 0.5t = 9.4
0.5t = 0.6
t = 1.2 hours = 72 minutes
```

---

**Step 3 — Cascade trace at t = 72 min**

Because all 5 cables are **identical in every parameter**, they all degrade to capacity = 9.4 tons **simultaneously** at t = 72 min.

The first cable to fail triggers redistribution — but the surviving cables have already hit their failure threshold:

| Survivors | New load per cable | Capacity at t=72 min | Holds? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | 47 ÷ 4 = **11.75 t** | 9.4 t | **No** — instant failure |
| 3 | 47 ÷ 3 = **15.67 t** | 9.4 t | **No** |
| 2 | 47 ÷ 2 = **23.5 t** | 9.4 t | **No** |
| 1 | **47 t** | 9.4 t | **No** |

The cascade is total and instantaneous once the first cable fails.

---

**The bridge collapses 72 minutes after Cable 1 snaps.**
